commit.message:
Fix libgpiod line ownership bug in GPIO functions (#134)

The libgpiod implementation had a critical resource management issue:
gpio_direction() acquired a line via gpiod_line_request but never
released it, causing gpio_write() and gpio_read() to fail when they
tried to re-request the same line.

Fix by caching line objects in a static array. gpio_direction()
releases and re-requests to change direction, while gpio_read()
and gpio_write() reuse the already-requested line directly.
gpio_unexport() now properly releases cached lines.

This fixes GPIO failures on Raspberry Pi with kernel 6.6+ when
built with libgpiod support.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add missing multi-byte command handlers to SDL emulators

Previously, unhandled multi-byte commands (e.g. 0xD5 Clock Divide on
SSD1306) would cause their argument bytes to be misinterpreted as new
commands, potentially corrupting emulator state.

SSD1306: Add 0x20, 0x8D, 0xD5, 0xD9, 0xDA, 0xDB (1-arg), 0x26/0x27
  (6-arg scroll), 0x29/0x2A (5-arg scroll)
SSD1331: Add 0x81-0x83, 0x87, 0x8A-0x8C, 0xB1, 0xB3, 0xB6, 0xBB,
  0xBE, 0x26 (1-arg), 0x22 (10-arg rect), 0x24/0x25 (4-arg),
  0x27 (5-arg scroll)
SSD1351: Add 0xA1, 0xA2, 0xAB, 0xB1, 0xB3, 0xB5, 0xB6, 0xBB, 0xBE,
  0xC7, 0xCA, 0xFD (1-arg), 0xC1 (3-arg contrast)
ILI9341: Add 0x26, 0x3A, 0x51, 0x53, 0x55, 0xC7, 0xF6 (1-arg),
  0xB1, 0xB5, 0xB6, 0xC0, 0xC1, 0xC5 (2-arg), 0xCF (3-arg),
  0xCB (5-arg), 0xE0/0xE1 (15-arg gamma)

commit.message:
Fix segfault on Ctrl+C in SDL emulator demos

Three issues caused the crash:

1. sdl_graphics_close() only destroyed the SDL window, leaking the
   renderer, texture, and pixel buffer. Now properly destroys all
   resources in reverse creation order with NULL guards.

2. SDL_QUIT event handler called exit(0) without cleanup. Now calls
   sdl_core_close() before exiting to properly release SDL resources.

3. No signal handler for SIGINT/SIGTERM. Ctrl+C would terminate the
   process mid-render, causing use-after-free in SDL cleanup. Added
   sdl_signal_handler that calls sdl_core_close() then _exit(0).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix SDL emulator state leaks between tests

- Add reset() functions to ILI9341, SSD1325, SSD1351, PCD8544 emulators
  to clear stale 'detected' flags between test runs
- Call unregister_oleds() at start of sdl_core_init() to prevent
  p_oled_db array from growing on repeated init calls
- Move ILI9341 s_firstStart from function-local to file-scope static
  so reset() can reach it
- Zero g_pixels buffer after malloc in sdl_graphics_set_oled_params()
  to prevent uninitialized memory reads
- Fix SH1107 emulator: multiplexRatio check >= to > (off-by-one
  disabled last display row)

Root cause: stale 'detected=1' flags in emulators without reset()
caused wrong display driver to win auto-detection when tests ran
in sequence, leading to buffer overflows (320x240 ILI9341 params
applied to 128x128 IL9163 buffer).

Add comprehensive step selection, clipboard, find & replace, and insert system

Implements stepper-step-select.js with:
- Step selection (click, Ctrl+click toggle, Shift+click range)
- DOM-to-data mapping with MutationObserver auto-refresh
- Clipboard: copy, cut, paste, delete with undo snapshots
- Find & Replace panel with case toggle, match navigation
- Insert step/section operations with templates
- Format operations (bold, italic, underline, strikethrough)
- Toast notification system with queue and auto-dismiss
- Keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+A/C/X/V/F/H, Delete, Escape)
- Full dark mode support via CSS injection
- Public API on window.__stepperStepSelect

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: anthonytau4 <250823307+anthonytau4@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: anthonytau4 <250823307+anthonytau4@users.noreply.github.com>

Rewrite hamburger nav as premium slide-out drawer with glassmorphism

Transforms the simple dropdown hamburger menu into an animated slide-out
navigation drawer with:

- Hamburger button with CSS bar-to-X morph animation and glassmorphism
- Full-height frosted-glass drawer sliding from the right (cubic-bezier)
- App branding header with gradient text and user info display
- Real-time search bar filtering menu items
- Menu items organized in labeled sections (Create, Discover, Tools, Account)
- Item descriptions, staggered entrance animation, ripple click effects
- Active item indicated with animated gradient left border
- Recently visited pages shown as pills (sessionStorage, max 3)
- Quick actions footer (dark mode toggle, fullscreen, keyboard shortcuts)
- Full keyboard navigation (arrows, Escape, Tab focus trap)
- Dark/light mode via html.dark class selectors
- @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) support
- CSS custom properties for easy theming
- Same IIFE pattern, install guard, public API, tab-strip hiding, and
  menu item actions as the original

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: robust ACP executable resolution with multi-strategy fallback

Replace fragile where/which-only validation in AcpAdapter with
multi-strategy path resolution via acpPathResolver.ts:

1. Check absolute path existence (with Windows .cmd/.exe extension)
2. Try system PATH via where/which
3. Scan common npm/node install locations per platform:
   - Windows: C:\.tools\.npm-global, %APPDATA%\npm, etc.
   - macOS: /usr/local/bin, /opt/homebrew/bin, etc.
   - Linux: ~/.npm-global/bin, /usr/local/bin, etc.

When found via fallback scan, auto-updates adapter executable to
resolved absolute path. Session-level cache avoids repeated lookups.
Failure includes full diagnostic report (checked paths, existence).

Also solidified troubleshooting knowledge into:
- system-instructions.md: ACP Engine Troubleshooting section
- mcp-servers.json: PATH inheritance documentation
- Project memory: architecture-decision entry

commit.message:
security: harden checkout steps and fix module verification

- Add persist-credentials: false to all three checkout steps
  (govulncheck, gosec, trivy) to prevent the GITHUB_TOKEN from
  persisting in the git config, reducing credential exposure.

- Replace misleading 'Verify go.sum' step (which silently succeeds on
  an empty module cache) with 'Download and verify modules' that runs
  go mod download && go mod verify, ensuring checksums are actually
  validated against go.sum.

- Add the same download-and-verify step to the gosec job since CI jobs
  run on isolated runners with independent caches.

feat: prompt cache optimization for parallel worker spawns

Split monolithic prompts into shared prefix + unique suffix to leverage
LLM API prompt caching. When multiple workers are spawned in parallel
(e.g. council dispatch), the shared prefix (system instructions, memory,
project context) is cached once and reused across all workers.

Implementation:
- PromptCacheManager: splits at 'Task Description:' boundary, SHA256
  cache key, TTL eviction (30min), hit tracking with stats
- AcpAdapter: sends multi-block content with cache_control ephemeral
  on shared prefix, with triple fallback chain (multi-block → single
  block → text string) for backward compatibility
- genericExecutor: passes promptParts through ExecuteOptions
- worker-spawner: splits basePrompt and passes promptParts to executor

Typical savings: 10-15KB shared prefix cached across 6 council members,
only ~0.5-1KB unique suffix processed per additional worker.

feat: lifecycle hooks system for task execution pipeline

Add a lifecycle hook registry inspired by Claude Code's hook architecture.
Hooks fire at 16 strategic points across the execution pipeline:

Task Lifecycle (council-runner.ts):
- PreTaskSpawn, TaskStarted, PreDelegation, PostDelegation
- PreSynthesis, TaskCompleted

Execution Phases (worker-spawner.ts):
- PreRoleResolution, PreSkillLoad, PrePromptConstruct
- PreExecute, PostExecute, ValidationGate, LoopDetection

MCP Dispatch (mcp-server.ts):
- PreToolDispatch, PostToolDispatch

Key design decisions:
- Best-effort: hook errors are caught and logged, never block execution
- Fire-and-forget: hooks use .catch(() => {}) pattern
- Veto support: Pre* hooks can return { veto: true } to block operations
- Singleton registry via getGlobalHookRegistry()
- Built-in hooks: taskMetricsHook, slowExecutionHook, loggingHook
